description: Reconcile two inventories -- e.g. FortiCloud/FortiCare assets vs a CMDB such as ServiceNow -- by a join key (typically a serial number), and persist the result as an asset-centric model: one recon_assets record per reconciled serial, with one or more recon_issues records (a missing-in-A, missing-in-B, field-mismatch, or license-expiring finding) linked to it. The playbook upserts by natural key (assets by join_key, issues by issue_key = join_key + mismatch_type) so re-runs update findings instead of duplicating, then emails a CSV report. The archetype fixes the two-module schema (assets parent, issues child + relationship), the connector roles, and the playbook flow; the router/agent fills the deployment-specific slots (module names, join key, recipients, schedule). First pilot instance: FortiCloud assets vs ServiceNow CMDB.
name: reconcile-and-report

parameters.description: Name of the FortiSOAR module that holds one record per reconciled asset (parent).
parameters.name: assets_module

parameters.description: Name of the FortiSOAR module that holds one record per reconciliation finding (child, linked to the asset).
parameters.name: issues_module

parameters.description: Human label for source A (e.g. 'FortiCloud assets').
parameters.name: source_a_label

parameters.description: Human label for source B (e.g. 'ServiceNow CMDB').
parameters.name: source_b_label

parameters.description: Field used to match records across the two sources (typically a serial number). Inferred from the module schema when possible, else prompted. Becomes the assets natural key and the upsert sourceId.
parameters.name: join_key

parameters.description: Email recipient(s) for the CSV report (comma-separated).
parameters.name: recipients

parameters.description: Cron schedule for the out-of-band reconciliation run.
parameters.name: cron

playbook_skeletons.description: Fetch both sources, diff by the join key, upsert every asset (by join_key) and every issue (by issue_key, linked to its asset), and email a CSV report. Abstract trigger (fired by an out-of-band schedule). Re-runs are idempotent: upsert + natural-key uniqueness update existing records instead of duplicating.
playbook_skeletons.name: Reconcile and Report

source.basis: ServiceNow + FortiCare reconciliation pilot (mutable-yawning-fox plan, Form 1)
source.note: Hand-curated (no single reconciliation solution pack exists to harvest). Asset-centric two-module model: recon_assets (parent, natural key join_key) + recon_issues (child, natural key issue_key = join_key + mismatch_type) with an `asset` lookup linking each issue to its asset. Module field types grounded in real mmd.json (string/text, picklists/picklist, datetime, lookup -> target module type). Dedup is two-layered: the module uniqueConstraint (built at publish) is the DB backstop; the playbook upserts via /api/3/upsert/<module> + sourceId=<natural key> + operation=Overwrite so re-runs update instead of duplicating (the data_ingest ruleset pattern). The diff step uses the custom 'code-runner' connector (role 'compute') instead of the stock 'code-snippet': code-snippet's sandbox execs at module level (no top-level return) and restricts open() (no CSV file write), which broke this archetype's natural design; code-runner removes both restrictions. code-runner is UNRESTRICTED arbitrary-code execution with filesystem access -- a deliberate trade-off for this trusted, operator-authored archetype. Source: connector-code-runner repo.

when_to_use: Compare two data sources, surface records present in one but not the other plus field mismatches on a join key, persist each reconciled asset and its findings to a parent asset / child issue record model (upserted by natural key so re-runs do not duplicate), and notify stakeholders with a CSV report. Triggered on a schedule (an out-of-band Celery task pointing at an abstract-trigger playbook), not by an inbound event.
